Functional Modules.Function Description: Provides user interaction and visual feedback, consisting of input controls and a display.
Functional Modules.Signal Sources: Tactile button physical presses (for power, volume up/down, station seek).
Functional Modules.Signal Destinations: Digital input signals to MCU GPIO pins, Visual output via OLED screen.
Functional Modules.Implementation Logic: Momentary push buttons are connected to MCU GPIO pins with pull-up resistors. The OLED display (I2C or SPI interface) is driven by the MCU to show frequency, volume level, battery status, and station information.
Functional Modules.Signal Chain: The UI & Control module's signal chain begins with user input from three tactile buttons (power, volume up/down, station seek). Each button connects to a dedicated MCU GPIO pin configured as a digital input with an internal pull-up resistor. When a button is pressed, it pulls the corresponding GPIO pin to ground, generating a low-level digital signal. The MCU continuously polls or uses interrupts to detect these state changes. For output, the MCU drives an OLED display via the I2C interface. The MCU acts as the I2C master, sending commands and pixel data over the SDA and SCL lines to the SSD1306BZ controller to update the display content (frequency, volume, battery status, station info). The display is powered by the 3.3V rail. The MCU itself is powered by the same 3.3V rail, which is generated by an LDO regulator from a higher voltage input (e.g., battery or USB). The audio path from the FM tuner and amplification is handled externally by other modules.

Recommended Components.Recommendation Reason: Acts as the central processor for reading button inputs and controlling the OLED display via I2C. Its available GPIO pins and I2C peripheral match the module requirements.
Provides the required monochrome OLED display for user feedback. Its I2C interface allows simple connection to the MCU with minimal wiring.
Provides a stable 3.3V supply from a higher input voltage (e.g., battery or USB) to power the MCU and OLED display, ensuring reliable operation within their voltage ranges.

Functional Modules.Function Description: Provides stable power to all system components, manages battery charging from USB Type-C input, and handles power source switching between battery and USB.
Functional Modules.Signal Sources: USB Type-C input (5V DC), Lithium-ion battery, Power switch signal.
Functional Modules.Signal Destinations: Linear voltage regulator output (stable VCC rail), Battery charging control, System power enable.
Functional Modules.Implementation Logic: A dedicated charging IC safely charges the Li-ion battery from the USB-C port. A power path management circuit selects between battery and USB power. A linear voltage regulator (e.g., LDO) takes the selected input (typically ~3.7V-5V) and outputs a clean, stable voltage (e.g., 3.3V) for sensitive digital logic.
Functional Modules.Signal Chain: The USB Type-C connector provides VBUS (5V) and ground. The VBUS line feeds into the MCP73831T charger IC's VCC pin as the primary charging input. The charger IC regulates the current and voltage to safely charge a single-cell Li-ion battery connected to its BAT pin. The battery voltage (typically ~3.7V) and the USB VBUS (5V) are inputs to a power path management function (implied by the module's logic; a simple diode-OR circuit or ideal diode controller would be needed but is not in the library). The selected higher voltage (either battery or USB) becomes the input to the ME6209 LDO regulator's VIN pin. The LDO outputs a stable 3.3V on its VOUT pin, which serves as the VCC rail for the system, including the ATtiny MCU's VDD pin and other peripherals requiring 3.3V. The ATtiny MCU can monitor the system via its GPIOs (e.g., read a power switch signal, monitor charger status via the CHRG pin) and control power enables. The charger IC's PROG pin is set with a resistor to ground to define charge current, and its CHRG status pin can be read by the MCU.

Recommended Components.Recommendation Reason: Provides the 5V DC input from a USB Type-C cable as the primary power source for charging and system operation.
Linear charger IC for single-cell Li-ion battery, compliant with USB power, providing safe constant-current/constant-voltage charging from the USB 5V input.
Low-dropout linear regulator to provide a stable 3.3V rail from the battery or USB input voltage for powering sensitive digital logic like the MCU.
MCU to monitor power status (e.g., read charger status CHRG), manage power enable signals, and handle system control logic.

Functional Modules.Function Description: Receives FM radio signals, locks onto a user-selected frequency, demodulates it, and outputs a low-level analog audio signal.
Functional Modules.Signal Sources: FM antenna input (RF signal), Digital control commands from MCU (e.g., I2C).
Functional Modules.Signal Destinations: Low-level analog audio output to audio conditioning circuit, Digital status/data (e.g., signal strength, RDS) to MCU.
Functional Modules.Implementation Logic: A dedicated digital FM tuner IC (e.g., Silicon Labs Si4703) receives RF via an antenna. The MCU sends frequency tuning commands digitally. The IC performs all RF processing (amplification, mixing, filtering) and demodulation digitally, outputting a clean analog audio line-level signal.
Functional Modules.Signal Chain: The FM antenna receives RF signals and feeds them into the RDA5807's FMIN pin. The RDA5807, powered by a regulated 3.3V supply from the ME6209, digitally tunes, demodulates, and decodes the FM signal. It is controlled via I2C by the ATtiny MCU, which sends frequency selection commands and reads status (e.g., signal strength, RDS data). The RDA5807 outputs low-level analog stereo audio (LOUT, ROUT). This audio is amplified by the TC8871 audio power amplifier to drive a speaker or headphone. The ATtiny also controls the SSD1306BZ OLED display via I2C to show frequency, volume, and other status. System power is provided via the USB Type-C connector, which supplies VBUS. The MCP73831T manages single-cell Li-ion battery charging when USB power is present, and the ME6209 LDO regulates the input to a stable 3.3V for the digital and analog circuits.

Recommended Components.Recommendation Reason: Fully integrated digital FM tuner IC that receives, tunes, demodulates, and outputs analog audio. It supports the required frequency range (50-115 MHz) and is controlled via I2C, matching the MCU interface.
MCU with I2C peripheral to control the RDA5807 (tuning, reading status) and SSD1306BZ display. Provides GPIO for user inputs and system management.
LDO regulator to provide stable 3.3V power from USB/battery input to the RDA5807, ATtiny, SSD1306BZ, and other low-power circuits.
OLED display module with I2C interface to show tuning frequency, volume, station info, and other status under MCU control.
Amplifies the low-level analog audio from the RDA5807 to drive a speaker or headphones, with selectable Class AB/D operation for efficiency/quality balance.
USB Type-C connector for system power input (VBUS) and potential future data communication. Provides robust power delivery up to 20V/5A.
Manages charging of a single-cell Li-ion battery from the USB power source, providing constant-current/constant-voltage charging with thermal regulation.

Functional Modules.Function Description: Conditions the demodulated audio signal and amplifies it to a level sufficient to drive a loudspeaker.
Functional Modules.Signal Sources: Low-level analog audio signal from FM tuner IC.
Functional Modules.Signal Destinations: Amplified audio signal to loudspeaker terminals.
Functional Modules.Implementation Logic: The tuner's audio output first passes through a passive RC high-pass filter to block DC offset. This conditioned signal is then fed into a class-D or class-AB audio power amplifier IC. The amplifier, potentially with volume control via the MCU (digital potentiometer or amplifier gain control), boosts the signal to drive an 8Ω or 4Ω speaker.
Functional Modules.Signal Chain: The audio processing and amplification module receives a low-level analog audio signal from the FM tuner IC (RDA5807). This signal first passes through a passive RC high-pass filter to block any DC offset and condition the audio. The conditioned signal is then fed into the audio power amplifier IC (TC8871). The amplifier, which can operate in Class AB or Class D mode, boosts the signal to a level sufficient to drive a loudspeaker (typically 4Ω or 8Ω). Volume control can be implemented via the MCU (ATtiny) by adjusting the amplifier's gain, potentially through a digital potentiometer (not in library) or by using the amplifier's shutdown/mode control pins for basic on/off and class selection. The MCU also controls the FM tuner via I2C for station selection and audio source management. Power is supplied from a USB Type-C connector, regulated down to appropriate voltages for the tuner, amplifier, and MCU by an LDO (ME6209) and potentially a battery charger (MCP73831T) if a battery is present. The display (SSD1306BZ) can show audio status and is controlled via I2C by the MCU.

Recommended Components.Recommendation Reason: Provides the low-level analog audio signal output (LOUT, ROUT) that serves as the input source for the amplification chain. Its I2C interface allows MCU control for tuning.
Amplifies the conditioned audio signal to drive a loudspeaker. Supports Class AB/D selection and has shutdown control, suitable for the module's power and audio quality requirements.
MCU to control the FM tuner via I2C, manage amplifier shutdown/mode, and potentially implement volume control logic. Provides necessary digital control for the audio processing chain.
Provides a stable 3.3V supply for the FM tuner (RDA5807), MCU (ATtiny), and other low-voltage components from a higher input voltage (e.g., from USB or battery).
USB Type-C connector provides primary power input (VBUS) to the system and can support battery charging via a charger IC.
If the system includes a single-cell Li-ion battery, this IC manages charging from the USB input, providing power to the system via the BAT pin when needed.
OLED display to show audio status (e.g., volume, station) controlled by the MCU via I2C, enhancing user interface.

Functional Modules.Function Description: Acts as the system brain, orchestrating all operations. It reads user inputs, controls the FM tuner, updates the display, and manages system states.
Functional Modules.Signal Sources: Tactile button inputs (digital), Digital communication bus from FM tuner (e.g., I2C data).
Functional Modules.Signal Destinations: Digital commands to FM tuner IC (e.g., I2C), Display data to OLED screen (e.g., I2C/SPI), Control signals to audio amplifier (e.g., enable/shutdown).
Functional Modules.Implementation Logic: A low-power microcontroller (e.g., ARM Cortex-M0+) runs firmware. It debounces button presses, interprets commands (volume/station change), communicates with the tuner IC via a digital bus to set frequency, fetches RDS data, and renders UI (frequency, volume, station info) on the OLED display.
Functional Modules.Signal Chain: The system operates as follows: Power is supplied via the USB Type-C connector (VBUS, 5V). The MCP73831T battery charger IC manages charging of a single-cell Li-ion battery from this USB power source, outputting battery voltage (typically 4.2V when charged). The ME6209 LDO regulator steps down the battery voltage (or USB voltage when present) to a stable 3.3V supply for the digital and analog components. The ATtiny MCU is powered by this 3.3V rail. The MCU reads digital inputs from tactile buttons connected to its GPIO pins (PA3, PA6, PA7). It controls the RDA5807 FM tuner via an I2C bus (using PA1 as SDA and PA2 as SCL) to set frequency, read RDS data, and control audio functions. The MCU also drives the SSD1306BZ OLED display via a separate I2C bus (using the same PA1/SDA and PA2/SCL pins, shared with the tuner, requiring careful firmware multiplexing or a dedicated bus; note: the library only provides one I2C peripheral on these pins, so they must be shared). The RDA5807 outputs stereo analog audio (LOUT, ROUT) to the TC8871 audio amplifier. The MCU controls the amplifier's shutdown (SD pin) and mode selection (MODE pin) via GPIOs (e.g., PA0 and PA7, but PA7 is used for a button; reassignment needed). The amplifier then drives a speaker in a bridge-tied-load (BTL) configuration. The MCU firmware orchestrates all operations: debouncing buttons, interpreting commands, communicating with the tuner, updating the display, and managing audio control.

Recommended Components.Recommendation Reason: Low-power 8-bit AVR MCU with I2C, sufficient I/O for buttons, and control of peripherals. Fits the role of system brain.
Manages single-cell Li-ion battery charging from USB input, providing safe charging and power path.
Provides stable 3.3V supply from battery or USB for MCU, tuner, display, and other low-voltage components.
Single-chip FM tuner with I2C control, RDS support, and integrated audio DACs, suitable for portable radio.
Monochrome OLED display with I2C interface for showing frequency, volume, and station info.
Class AB/D selectable mono amplifier for driving a speaker from the tuner's audio output, with shutdown control.
USB Type-C connector for power input and potential firmware updates via UPDI (if multiplexed).
